creditsExplainedPage.subtitle: Everything you need to know about how credits work on Kortix

understandingCredits.title: Understanding Credits
understandingCredits.description: Credits serve as Kortix's universal currency for platform operations. Every action your AI workers perform—from analyzing data to generating code—consumes credits based on the task's complexity and the resources required.

howCreditsWork.title: How Credits Work
howCreditsWork.description: Credits are consumed based on the resources your AI workers use:

aiModelUsage.title: AI Model Usage
aiModelUsage.description: The primary driver of credit consumption
aiModelUsage.content: Different AI models have different costs based on their capabilities and token usage. Credits are consumed for input tokens (your prompts and context), output tokens (Worker responses), and vary by model tier (Claude, GPT, etc.).

pricingModel.title: Pricing
pricingModel.description: Platform rates vary by service type
pricingModel.content: We apply a markup on top of provider costs to cover platform infrastructure, security, and ongoing development. AI models have a 20% markup, tool usage (web search, scraping, etc.) has a 50% markup, image search has a 100% markup, and if you use your own API key, a flat 10% platform fee applies instead.

gettingMoreCredits.title: Getting More Credits
gettingMoreCredits.description: There are several ways to obtain credits in Kortix:

monthlySubscription.title: Monthly Subscription Credits
monthlySubscription.description: Included with your paid plan and renewed automatically each month. These are expiring credits.

topUpCredits.title: Top-Up Credits
topUpCredits.description: Purchase additional credits when you need them. These are non-expiring and available to premium members.

promotionalGrants.title: Promotional & Event Grants
promotionalGrants.description: Bonus credits from special events, promotions, or referrals. These are non-expiring.

refunds.title: Refunds
refunds.description: Credits returned due to technical issues or failed tasks. These are non-expiring.

typesOfCredits.title: Types of Credits
typesOfCredits.description: Kortix uses two types of credits to give you flexibility in how you manage your usage:

expiringCredits.title: Expiring Credits
expiringCredits.content: These credits are included with your paid subscription and are renewed automatically each month on your subscription date. They expire at the end of each billing cycle and are always consumed first before any non-expiring credits.

nonExpiringCredits.title: Non-Expiring Credits
nonExpiringCredits.content: These credits never expire and carry over month to month. They include top-up purchases, refunds, and promotional grants. Non-expiring credits are only used after your expiring credits have been depleted.

creditPriority.title: Credit Priority:
creditPriority.description: When you use Kortix, expiring credits are consumed first. Only after your expiring credits run out will non-expiring credits be used.

appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line69JsxTextNotToolsNotAgentsNotWorkflo: Not tools. Not agents. Not workflows. Companies — with engineering departments, operations teams, finance functions, customer support, growth engines — that run themselves. Agents doing the actual work, 24/7, without a human touching every task.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line93JsxTextThisIsNotAForecastItIsOur: This is not a forecast. It is our operating target — the ratio we run ourselves at today, and the ratio we build toward with every company we work with. Humans are not removed. They are elevated. They set direction, review outputs, make final calls on things that matter. Agents handle the rest.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line111JsxTextAFactoryIsASystemForTurnin: A factory is a system for turning inputs into outputs at scale, reliably, repeatedly, without depending on any individual person being present. That is what we build for companies.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line118JsxTextYouFeedTheFactoryYourGoals: You feed the factory your goals, your tools, your context, your standards. The factory produces: shipped code, closed tickets, drafted contracts, reconciled books, outbound campaigns, synthesized research, recruited candidates. Every day. Whether you are in the office or asleep.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line138JsxAttrBodyEveryCompanyHasProcess: Every company has processes that run on human attention. We find them — support queues, reporting cycles, outbound sequences, QA checks, code review, invoice processing — and make them legible. You cannot automate what you have not named.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line143JsxAttrBodyEachProcessBecomesAnAg: Each process becomes an agent. Not a chatbot. Not a prompt wrapper. A specialist — with its own identity, permissions, tools, memory, and trigger rules. An engineering agent that ships PRs. A finance agent that closes the books. A support agent that resolves tickets before a human sees them.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line148JsxAttrBodyAgentsAreOnlyAsPowerfu: Agents are only as powerful as the tools they can reach. We wire the entire company stack — codebases, CRMs, databases, communication platforms, file systems, external APIs. An agent that cannot act is just a language model. An agent that can reach everything is an employee.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line153JsxTextGoalLoopTheAutonomousExecu: Goal loop: the autonomous execution loop. An agent takes a task, works it through to completion, self-verifies the result, and only stops when it can prove the output is correct. No hand-holding. No approval at every step. The job either gets done or the agent escalates with a clear explanation of why.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line158JsxAttrBodyEverySessionEveryDecis: Every session, every decision, every correction is retained. Agents remember what worked. They remember your preferences, your standards, your edge cases. The longer the factory runs, the smarter it gets. This is the compounding advantage humans cannot replicate at scale.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line163JsxAttrBodyTheFinalLayerIsHumanGo: The final layer is human governance: reviewing what matters, correcting course, setting new objectives. Not touching every task. Not approving every output. Governing the system. This is the 24% — and it is the most leveraged 24% these people will ever spend.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line178JsxTextWeRunOurOwnCompanyOnKortix: We run our own company on Kortix. Every day. Our engineering, our operations, our growth — agents doing the work, humans governing the system. We eat our own output before we ship it to anyone else.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line186JsxTextThisIsNotAProductDemoItIs: This is not a product demo. It is how we operate. The credibility of the playbook comes from the fact that we are living inside it. Every failure we encounter, we fix. Every edge case we hit, we document. The factory improves itself.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line203JsxTextKortixIsInfrastructureTheP: Kortix is infrastructure. The platform is not the point.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line209JsxTextThePointIsTheMigrationFrom: The point is the migration — from human-operated to AI-operated. From companies that require constant human attention to keep running, to companies that run themselves and only require human judgment to keep improving.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line217JsxTextWeThinkEverySeriousCompany: We think every serious company will make this migration in the next few years. The ones that move early will be operating at a cost base and execution speed that latecomers cannot catch. We are building the path — and walking it first.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line228JsxTextWeAreASmallTeamWeCareThat: We are a small team. We care that the playbook is real, that it works, and that every company we help run this way comes out the other side faster and leaner than when they started.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line236JsxTextIfYouWantToBuildThisWithUs: If you want to build this with us —
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line243JsxTextIfYouWantToRunYourCompanyO: If you want to run your company on it —

appHomeTechnologyPage.line162JsxTextOneFrameworkToRunYourAiNat: One framework to run your AI-native company. Every agent, skill, trigger, and workflow is code in a single repository — versioned, reviewable, and yours. We call it the Git-First Company Framework.

appHelpCreditsPage.line34JsxTextLearnHowCreditsWorkAndHowTh: Learn how credits work and how they're consumed
appHelpCreditsPage.line42JsxTextCreditsAreKortixSStandardUn: Credits are Kortix's standard unit of measurement for platform usage. Think of them as tokens that power your AI agents - the more complex or lengthy the task, the more credits it requires.
appHelpCreditsPage.line49JsxTextKortixUsesTwoTypesOfCredits: Kortix uses two types of credits to give you flexibility in how you manage your usage:
appHelpCreditsPage.line69JsxTextTheseCreditsAreIncludedWith: These credits are included with your paid subscription and are renewed automatically each month on your subscription date. They expire at the end of each billing cycle and are always consumed first before any non-expiring credits.
appHelpCreditsPage.line92JsxTextTheseCreditsNeverExpireAndC: These credits never expire and carry over month to month. They include top-up purchases, refunds, and promotional grants. Non-expiring credits are only used after your expiring credits have been depleted.
appHelpCreditsPage.line103JsxTextWhenYouUseKortixExpiringCr: When you use Kortix, expiring credits are consumed first. Only after your expiring credits run out will non-expiring credits be used.
appHelpCreditsPage.line112JsxTextCreditsAreConsumedBasedOnT: Credits are consumed based on the resources your AI agents use:
appHelpCreditsPage.line125JsxTextThePrimaryDriverOfCreditCo: The primary driver of credit consumption
appHelpCreditsPage.line132JsxTextDifferentAiModelsHaveDiffe: Different AI models have different costs based on their capabilities and token usage. Credits are consumed for input tokens (your prompts and context), output tokens (agent responses), and vary by model tier (GPT-4, Claude, etc.).
appHelpCreditsPage.line148JsxTextPlatformRatesVaryByService: Platform rates vary by service type
appHelpCreditsPage.line156JsxTextWeApplyAMarkupOnTopOfProvi: We apply a markup on top of provider costs to cover platform infrastructure, security, and ongoing development. Rates vary by service type:
appHelpCreditsPage.line162JsxTextText20MarkupOnAllLlmApiCos: 20% markup on all LLM API costs (input/output tokens, prompt caching).
appHelpCreditsPage.line166JsxTextText50MarkupOnWebSearchWeb: 50% markup on web search, web scraping, and other third-party tool calls.
appHelpCreditsPage.line174JsxTextIfYouUseYourOwnApiKeyA: If you use your own API key, a flat 10% platform fee applies instead.
appHelpCreditsPage.line186JsxTextThereAreSeveralWaysToObtai: There are several ways to obtain credits in Kortix:
appHelpCreditsPage.line199JsxTextIncludedWithYourPaidPlanAn: Included with your paid plan and renewed automatically each month. These are expiring credits.
appHelpCreditsPage.line215JsxTextPurchaseAdditionalCreditsW: Purchase additional credits when you need them. These are non-expiring and available to premium members.
appHelpCreditsPage.line231JsxTextBonusCreditsFromSpecialEve: Bonus credits from special events, promotions, or referrals. These are non-expiring.
appHelpCreditsPage.line247JsxTextCreditsReturnedDueToTechni: Credits returned due to technical issues or failed tasks. These are non-expiring.
appHelpCreditsPage.line259JsxTextMonitorYourCreditConsumpti: Monitor your credit consumption through the Settings panel:
appHelpCreditsPage.line267JsxTextViewYourCurrentCreditBalan: View your current credit balance and breakdown between expiring and non-expiring credits
appHelpCreditsPage.line275JsxTextTrackCreditConsumptionByTh: Track credit consumption by thread and conversation to identify your most resource-intensive chats
appHelpCreditsPage.line285JsxTextMakeYourCreditsGoFurtherWi: Make your credits go further with these optimization strategies:
appHelpCreditsPage.line293JsxTextUseSmallerMoreEfficientMod: Use smaller, more efficient models for simpler tasks. Save advanced models like GPT-4 for complex reasoning.

componentsIamApprovalsCard.line117JsxTextWhenOnTheCuratedSetOfHighB: When on, the curated set of high-blast-radius IAM actions requires approval from a second super-admin before they execute. Requesters can't approve their own requests.

componentsIamAuditWebhooksCard.line103JsxTextShipEveryAuditEventToYourS: Ship every audit event to your SIEM or generic HTTP endpoint. Payloads are signed with HMAC-SHA256.
componentsIamAuditWebhooksCard.line400JsxTextOnlyDeliverEventsWhoseActi: Only deliver events whose action starts with this prefix. Leave blank to deliver everything.

componentsIamBreakGlassCard.line98JsxTextSelfActivateTemporarySuperA: Self-activate temporary super-admin for incident response. A reason is required and every activation, expiry, and revocation is audit-logged.
componentsIamBreakGlassCard.line134JsxTextNoBreakGlassGrantsOnFileAc: No break-glass grants on file. Activate one only during a real incident — every use shows up in audit reports.
componentsIamBreakGlassCard.line259JsxTextYouLlGetFullSuperAdminUnti: You'll get full super-admin until the grant expires or you revoke it. Every action you take while active is attributed normally — break-glass only adds permission, never anonymity.
componentsIamBreakGlassCard.line269JsxTextUseOnlyDuringARealIncident: Use only during a real incident. Activation is audit-logged with your name and reason.
componentsIamBreakGlassCard.line279JsxAttrPlaceholderEGP0BillingPipe: e.g. P0 — billing pipeline stuck, need to rotate secrets

componentsIamDriftCard.line115JsxAttrEmptyNoExpiredPoliciesToCl: No expired policies to clean up.
componentsIamDriftCard.line189JsxTextUseTheIAMAPIToEnumerateThe: . Use the IAM API to enumerate the rest.

componentsIamExternalGrantsCard.line72JsxTextGrantAConsultantOrPartnerFr: Grant a consultant or partner from a different Kortix account specific access here. They keep their own login; you attach policies normally on the member detail page.
componentsIamExternalGrantsCard.line91JsxTextNoExternalUsersAttachedUseT: No external users attached. Use this for consultants and partners who shouldn't consume a regular seat.
componentsIamExternalGrantsCard.line162JsxAttrDescriptionTheUserImmediat: The user immediately loses access to this account. Their own account is unaffected.
componentsIamExternalGrantsCard.line213JsxTextTheUserMustAlreadyHaveAKor: The user must already have a Kortix account. Look them up by email; you'll attach policies on their member page after.

componentsIamMfaRequiredCard.line119JsxTextWhenEnabledMembersMustComp: When enabled, members must complete a second-factor challenge before any IAM-gated action. Super-admins and Personal Access Tokens are exempt.
componentsIamMfaRequiredCard.line167JsxTextMembersWithoutAVerifiedSec: Members without a verified second factor will be blocked from every IAM-gated action until they enrol. CLI tokens (PATs) are unaffected.
componentsIamMfaRequiredCard.line185JsxTextNobodyWouldRetainAccessPro: Nobody would retain access. Promote a super-admin or have at least one member enrol MFA before enabling.
componentsIamMfaRequiredCard.line208JsxTextWillBeLockedOutUntilTheyEn: will be locked out until they enrol MFA:
componentsIamMfaRequiredCard.line234JsxTextWithoutMFATheyWonTBeLocked: without MFA — they won't be locked out, but consider asking them to enrol:
componentsIamMfaRequiredCard.line283JsxTextMembersWillBeAbleToSignInW: Members will be able to sign in with a password alone. Per-policy MFA conditions you may have set on individual policies still apply — only the account-wide gate is removed.

componentsIamPatPolicyCard.line98JsxTextApplyToPersonalAccessTokens: Apply to Personal Access Tokens (CLI / programmatic clients). Sandbox-injected tokens are exempt — their lifetime is bound to the sandbox itself.
componentsIamPatPolicyCard.line120JsxTextWhenOnTheMintEndpointRefus: When on, the mint endpoint refuses PATs without an
componentsIamPatPolicyCard.line137JsxTextRefusesPATsWhoseRequestedE: Refuses PATs whose requested expires_at is further out than this many days from now.
componentsIamPatPolicyCard.line151JsxTextTokensNotUsedInThisManyDay: Tokens not used in this many days are auto-revoked on the next sign-in attempt.

componentsIamPermissionBoundaryCard.line111JsxAttrDescriptionCapsTheMaxSetOf: Caps the max set of actions this member can ever use, no matter how many allow-policies they have. Super-admins are exempt.
componentsIamPermissionBoundaryCard.line120JsxTextNoBoundaryConfiguredAllExp: No boundary configured. All explicit allow-policies apply at full strength.
componentsIamPermissionBoundaryCard.line127JsxTextBoundaryIsAnEmptyListThisM: Boundary is an empty list — this member is effectively denied every action until you add prefixes or clear the boundary.
componentsIamPermissionBoundaryCard.line134JsxTextAllowedActionPrefixesUseAT: Allowed action prefixes. Use a trailing dot to cover a whole namespace (e.g.
componentsIamPermissionBoundaryCard.line234JsxAttrDescriptionRemovesTheBound: Removes the boundary entirely. All explicit allow-policies on this member will apply again at full strength.

componentsIamProjectGroupsCard.line76JsxTextBundleProjectsSoOnePolicyCo: Bundle projects so one policy covers many — e.g. “Mobile editors get Editor role on every project in the Mobile group”. Pick the group as the scope when creating a policy.
componentsIamProjectGroupsCard.line95JsxTextNoProjectGroupsYetCreateOne: No project groups yet. Create one, attach projects, then use it as a scope in any policy.
componentsIamProjectGroupsCard.line231JsxTextChooseAShortDescriptiveNam: Choose a short, descriptive name. You'll add projects to the group next.
componentsIamProjectGroupsCard.line346JsxTextProjectsInThisGroupAreCove: Projects in this group are covered by any policy scoped to it.
componentsIamProjectGroupsCard.line391JsxTextEveryProjectInThisAccountI: Every project in this account is already in the group.

componentsIamScimCard.line99JsxTextConnectOktaAzureADOrAnySCIM: Connect Okta, Azure AD, or any SCIM 2.0 provider to sync users and groups into this account.
componentsIamScimCard.line132JsxTextEachTokenAuthenticatesASin: Each token authenticates a single IdP integration. Rotate by minting a new one and revoking the old.
componentsIamScimCard.line153JsxTextNoSCIMTokensYetCreateOneTo: No SCIM tokens yet. Create one to connect your IdP.
componentsIamScimCard.line338JsxTextUsedOnlyToRecogniseThisTok: Used only to recognise this token later.

componentsIamServiceAccountsCard.line96JsxTextMachineIdentitiesForCICDAnd: Machine identities for CI/CD and integrations. Attach policies just like a member — pick the service account as the principal when creating a policy.
componentsIamServiceAccountsCard.line115JsxTextNoServiceAccountsYetCreate: No service accounts yet. Create one to get a bearer token your CI/automation can use without impersonating a human.
componentsIamServiceAccountsCard.line289JsxTextABearerTokenWillBeShownOnc: A bearer token will be shown once after creation. Attach policies to it from the member detail view (it appears under the Token principal type).
componentsIamServiceAccountsCard.line362JsxTextThisIsTheOnlyTimeWeLlShow: This is the only time we'll show
componentsIamServiceAccountsCard.line362JsxTextSSecretStoreItInYourSecret: 's secret. Store it in your secrets manager — we can't recover it if you lose it.

componentsIamSessionControlsCard.line148JsxTextCapHowLongABrowserSessionL: Cap how long a browser session lives, force re-sign-in after idle periods, and force-logout specific sessions. Personal Access Tokens are not affected.
componentsIamSessionControlsCard.line170JsxTextForcesAFreshSignInAfterThi: Forces a fresh sign-in after this many minutes, measured from when the access token was issued.
componentsIamSessionControlsCard.line179JsxAttrPlaceholderLeaveBlankForNo: leave blank for no idle gate
componentsIamSessionControlsCard.line184JsxTextKillsTheSessionAfterThisMa: Kills the session after this many minutes of no activity against this account.
componentsIamSessionControlsCard.line218JsxTextNoSessionsTrackedYetSessio: No sessions tracked yet. Sessions show up here the first time a member hits an account-scoped route while a policy is configured.

componentsIamSsoCard.line106JsxTextConnectYourIdPUsersSigning: Connect your IdP. Users signing in via SAML are auto-provisioned and their IAM groups are kept in sync from a JWT claim.
componentsIamSsoCard.line127JsxTextNoSAMLProviderConfiguredSe: No SAML provider configured. Set up the IdP in Supabase Studio (Authentication → SSO) first, then paste the resulting provider UUID and your domain here.
componentsIamSsoCard.line172JsxTextNoMappingsYetMapIdPGroupRo: No mappings yet. Map IdP group/role values (from the
componentsIamSsoCard.line173JsxTextClaimToIAMGroupsSoUsersLan: claim) to IAM groups so users land in the right group on sign-in.
componentsIamSsoCard.line254JsxAttrDescriptionExistingMembers: Existing members keep their access; new sign-ins via SAML stop being provisioned and group sync no longer runs.
componentsIamSsoCard.line343JsxTextCreateTheProviderInSupabas: Create the provider in Supabase Studio (Authentication → SSO) first. Paste the resulting provider UUID below.
componentsIamSsoCard.line379JsxTextUsedToRouteSignInFlowsForT: Used to route sign-in flows for this domain through your IdP.
componentsIamSsoCard.line409JsxTextWhenOffOnlyUsersAnAdminHas: When off, only users an admin has already invited can sign in via SAML. Group sync still runs for those members.
componentsIamSsoCard.line492JsxTextUsersWithThisClaimValueInT: Users with this claim value in their SAML token will be added to the chosen IAM group on sign-in.
componentsIamSsoCard.line508JsxTextExactCaseSensitiveMatchAga: Exact, case-sensitive match against an entry in the group claim your IdP sends.
componentsIamSsoCard.line526JsxTextNoGroupsInThisAccountYetCr: No groups in this account yet. Create one first.

componentsIamStrictModeCard.line118JsxTextWhenEnabledOnlySuperAdminB: When enabled, only super-admin bypass and explicit IAM policies grant access. Legacy owner/admin/member roles and project_members rows are ignored.
componentsIamStrictModeCard.line162JsxTextTheLegacyBridgesOwnerAdmin: The legacy bridges (owner/admin/member roles, project_members rows) stop granting access. Only super-admin bypass and explicit IAM policies will work after this.
componentsIamStrictModeCard.line180JsxTextNobodyWouldRetainAccessPro: Nobody would retain access. Promote a super-admin or create at least one explicit policy before enabling.
componentsIamStrictModeCard.line190JsxTextNoMembersWillLoseAccessEve: No members will lose access — every member is super-admin or has an explicit policy.
componentsIamStrictModeCard.line226JsxAttrTitleMirrorLegacyAccountRo: Mirror legacy account_role + project_members rows into IAM policies. Safe to re-run.
componentsIamStrictModeCard.line266JsxTextLegacyBridgesResumeGrantin: Legacy bridges resume granting access alongside your explicit policies. Owners/admins regain Administrator privileges, members regain account-level reads, and project_members rows act as fallbacks again. Existing policies are not affected.
